title: Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift
hook: BatchNorm lets deep networks learn faster by calming shifting signals inside each layer.
promise: You will understand how Batch Normalization stabilizes and speeds up deep network training.
prerequisite_note: Bring an intuition for how neural networks learn layer by layer, and why shifting inputs can cause problems.

glossary.plain: The changing distribution of a layer’s inputs as earlier layers update during training.
The process of adjusting values so they fit within a certain range or distribution.
A small, randomly chosen subset of the training data processed together in one step.
A function like ReLU or sigmoid that lets neural networks model complex patterns.
A regularization method where random neurons are ignored during training to prevent overfitting.
A parameter that controls how much the model’s weights change in response to each update.

cards.title: Why deep learning sped up
body: Learn how a simple normalization trick made deep networks much easier and faster to train.

cards.title: Why training deep nets used to be so hard
body: Imagine trying to stack cards on a table while the table keeps wobbling. Each layer in a neural network faces a similar problem as it learns.
When earlier layers change, the inputs to later layers shift, making learning unstable and slow. This forces careful tuning and makes deep models hard to train.

cards.title: How BatchNorm changes the training flow
body: Before BatchNorm, each layer had to adjust to constantly shifting inputs, making training a balancing act.
BatchNorm normalizes the inputs of each layer for every mini-batch, keeping the signals steady as training progresses.
This stability lets networks use higher learning rates and train faster, often reaching better accuracy with less effort.
cards.caption: BatchNorm tames the chaos inside deep networks, leading to smoother and quicker learning.

cards.title: Training before and after BatchNorm
body: Watch what happens when you train a deep network with and without BatchNorm.
cards.offNote: Training is slow, learning rates must be low, and results are sensitive to initialization.
cards.onNote: Training is fast, higher learning rates work, and initialization is less critical.

cards.title: The magic of normalizing layer inputs
body: BatchNorm takes the outputs of each layer and rescales them so they have a mean of zero and a standard deviation of one, per mini-batch.
By making this normalization a standard part of the model, it reduces the shifting of inputs and lets each layer learn on a more stable foundation.

cards.title: How much normalization helps
body: Adjust the slider to see how varying the strength of normalization affects training speed and stability.
cards.low: Training is unstable and slow, requiring careful tuning.
cards.mid: Some stabilization, moderate speedup, but not optimal.
cards.high: Training is fast and stable, with high learning rates possible.

cards.title: What goes into BatchNorm?
body: Pick the ingredients that make BatchNorm work within a neural network.

choices.label: Mini-batch statistics
choices.note: Calculates the mean and variance for normalization.

choices.label: Learnable scale parameter
choices.note: Lets the model adjust the normalized output's scale.

choices.label: Learnable shift parameter
choices.note: Lets the model shift the normalized output up or down.

choices.label: Per-layer normalization
choices.note: Applies normalization to each layer separately.

choices.label: Nonlinearity after normalization
choices.note: Ensures the network keeps its expressive power.

choices.label: Backpropagation support
choices.note: Allows the normalization step to be trained end-to-end.

cards.title: BatchNorm’s impact on deep learning
body: BatchNorm let models reach the same accuracy in far fewer training steps, making deep learning practical for much larger models.
It also acted as a regularizer, sometimes removing the need for techniques like Dropout, and led to new benchmark-leading results.

bars.label: Training steps needed
bars.note: Fewer steps to reach target accuracy

cards.caption: BatchNorm accelerated training, improved accuracy, and simplified the deep learning workflow.

cards.title: Why BatchNorm is a classic
body: BatchNorm changed how researchers and practitioners train deep neural networks, making them faster, more robust, and easier to tune.
Its influence is felt in almost every modern deep learning architecture, and its core idea inspired many later normalization techniques.

recap.label: Normalize
recap.note: Standardize layer inputs using mini-batch statistics.

recap.label: Stabilize
recap.note: Reduce internal covariate shift for smoother training.

recap.label: Accelerate
recap.note: Allow higher learning rates and faster convergence.

cards.title: Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift
body: Read the original paper to see the experiments and details behind this influential normalization method.
